source.filename: Primary Sjogren's Syndrome (Mild Sicca Predominant) AMS-038.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 AMS (Autoimmune Mild Severity) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AMS casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-RHEUM-03-00238 — SEQ is provisional; ingest --reseq to confirm/assign the real sequence within the SPEC-RHEUM-03 bucket. Source note number AMS-038 recorded in source.original_case_id.

Patient age (~56) and sex (female) are INFERRED — the source note states only weight (68 kg) and vitals, not age or sex. Values chosen conservatively given the strong female predominance and typical mid-life onset of primary Sjögren's syndrome; confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context (RA1_major_city) inferred — the source note states only 'Private residence'; confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied and presentation de-anchored: the source note is an in-person clinician who examined the oropharynx, palpated the salivary glands, massaged Stensen's duct, examined the neck and observed a 30 mL water swallow to exclude a mechanical food-bolus impaction and acute suppurative sialadenitis. Breath-Ezy cannot examine or visualise the throat/glands, so the sealed disposition is a presumptive benign xerostomia friction flare reached from patient-reportable discriminators, plus robust safety-netting — NOT a confident remote exclusion. Physical-exam findings and measured vitals are sealed in nodes 10/11.

Honest tier discrimination: baseline set to T1 (self-care with palliative lubrication + routine 2–3 week follow-up), matching the source clinician's home-management disposition, with a prompt GP review (T2) noted as an acceptable more-cautious alternative because the throat cannot be visualised remotely; escalation edges encode T4 (ED) for food-bolus red flags, T5 (000) for airway compromise, T3 for suppurative sialadenitis features, and T2 for candidal complications / non-resolution.

Differential/comorbidity SNOMED and ICD-10-AM codes (notably 235602008 food-bolus obstruction, 45154002 sialadenitis, 41291007 angioedema) are best-effort candidates and may be imprecise; all registered unverified_pending_terminology_receipt for downstream batch terminology verification.

No doses synthesised: artificial saliva and mouthwash are used as-needed topical measures per the source note; pilocarpine is deferred to future GP/rheumatology review with no dose recorded (source gives none). No paediatric dosing involved (adult patient).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
